---
title: "data-exploration"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

data = read.csv("translation_data_possible_duplicates.csv")
```

book being part of one series at a time 
one target audience
review one book at a time 
one source lang, literatur
not one pub loc 


```{r}
data[duplicated(data),] |> nrow()
```

```{r}
translated_names <- c("record_type","target_audience", "title","author","original_title","genre","translator", "source_language", "source_literature","editor","author_of_foreword_or_afterword","place_of_publication","publisher", "year_of_publication", "physical_description", "edition_1", "series_information", "author_of_translated_book_under_review", "edition_2", "page_numbers", "content", "notes", "id") 

#Translated with DeepL.com (free version)
data_ed <- data
names(data_ed) <- translated_names
```


```{r}
# search also for those who are not expected to have ',' 
data_ed |> filter_all(any_vars(str_detect(., ';'))) |> View()

data_ed |> filter(str_detect(title, ';'))
data_ed |> filter(str_detect(title, ',')) |> View()

# data_ed |> filter(across(.fns = all(), str_detect(";")))
```

Variables with ',' indicating two or more records: 
- source language 
- source literature 
- genre


```{r}
data |> group_by(sihtrühm) |> count() 
```


```{r}
data |> group_by(teaviku_laad) |> count() 
```

```{r}
data |> group_by(autor) |> count()
```

```{r}
all_authors <- data$autor |> unique()
# all_authors[str_detect(all_authors,';')] # more authors in one record 
all_authors[str_detect(all_authors,'[0-9]')] # random
all_authors[str_detect(all_authors,'[0-9]') & !str_detect(all_authors,'[A-Z]') & !str_detect(all_authors,'[a-z]')]
all_authors[!str_detect(all_authors,'[0-9]')]
all_authors[str_length(all_authors) < 6]
all_authors[str_detect(all_authors,"-")]
all_authors[str_detect(all_authors,"\\(")]
all_authors[str_detect(all_authors,"\\[")]
all_authors[str_detect(all_authors,"\\?")]

#summary: 

#  weird things when auth < 6 
#  more authors when ; 
#  rather practical years
#  pseud
all_authors[str_length(all_authors) < 4 & str_detect(all_authors,"\\.")]
all_authors[str_length(all_authors) < 4 & str_detect(all_authors,"-")]
```




```{r}
length(data$id |> unique())
data |> nrow()
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
data |>  
  ggplot(aes(x=ilmumisaasta)) + geom_histogram()
```
